scope_note: QA Book 4 Synthesis: Platonic Edges, CRT Triple Uniqueness, Aliquot Minima cert [329]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1991) Quantum Arithmetic Volume II -- Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. pp.53-65: 'ALIQUOT PARTS', 'PAR TYPES', 'PLATONIC SOLIDS', 'PARAMETERS'. Five structural results certified: (C1) Platonic solid edge count: E = F*epf/2 (epf=edges_per_face) verified for all 5 solids -- tetrahedron(4,6,4,3), cube(8,12,6,4), octahedron(6,12,8,3), dodecahedron(20,30,12,5), icosahedron(12,30,20,3). Iverson p.61: 'The number of edges on any Platonic solid is equal to the number of edges per face multiplied by half the number of faces.' (C2) Euler formula V-E+F=2 holds for all 5 solids; face polygon types: triangle (3 solids), square (1), pentagon (1) -- same three polygons as harmonic cycle [327]. (C3) Triple classification (n mod 3, n mod 4, n mod 5) uniquely identifies n in {1..60} by CRT (lcm(3,4,5)=60; pairwise coprime); Iverson p.56 examples: 37=(1,1,2)=4-tri/5-par/7-pent, 32=(2,0,2)=2-tri/4-par/7-pent. (C4) Three smallest aliquot parts: 30=lcm(2,3,5), 42=lcm(2,3,7), 210=lcm(2,3,5,7); all divisible by 6; 210/30=7, 210/42=5 -- each smaller is aliquot of 210. (C5) Musical intervals (p.62): major third=5:4, major fifth=3:2, major seventh=15:8=5/4*3/2; all 7-smooth; major seventh = product of third and fifth. Builds on certs [327] (harmonic cycle) and [328] (aliquot parts). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
